prose:
Calculation details (method-provenance block)

Property: frontier orbital energies (HOMO/LUMO) and HOMO–LUMO gap, eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF backend)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates  
 • file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz  
 • single-point evaluation (no re-optimization at DFT)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
SCF convergence: converged in 8 cycles; maximum |ΔE| < 1 × 10⁻⁶ Ha
Engine log: streamed live to frontier_orbitals_<timestamp>.out in the working directory; JSON results written to …/tmp4ctbhg8p/live.json.
Warnings: no warnings reported by the engine.
Integrity check: status = ok, trustworthy = true.

Headline results
HOMO energy  = −7.0468329 eV  
LUMO energy  = −0.41574728 eV  
HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 eV  ← reported value

Additional orbital energies (eV)
  HOMO–2: −9.487467  
  HOMO–1: −7.0473344  
  LUMO+1: −0.41513054  
  LUMO+2:  1.2021883

Koopmans-derived reactivity indices
  Ionization potential     = 7.0468329 eV  
  Electron affinity        = 0.41574728 eV  
  Chemical hardness (η)    = 3.3155428 eV  
  Electronegativity (χ)    = 3.7312901 eV  
  Electrophilicity index   = 2.0995847 eV

Reproducibility
Command executed (see `cli_invocation` in JSON):
  chemkit frontier-orbitals --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/…/benzene.xyz --out …/live.json
Output files (.xyz, live .out log, result JSON, molden & cube files) are available in the indicated scratch directory.

No experimental comparison requested; values are purely computational.
